stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 4 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 5 observations with mean 60.1 % (within-factory SD = 9.9). factory B has 15 observations with mean 56.3 % (within-factory SD = 9.1). factory C has 20 observations with mean 67.0 % (within-factory SD = 7.4). factory D has 20 observations with mean 77.0 % (within-factory SD = 7.7). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 4.3 and 10.6 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory D: 76.5 % (shrunk from raw 77.0). Range [75.6, 76.7] as between-factory SD varies from 4.3 to 10.6. Pooled mean: 67.1.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 67.1 % (weighted average of 4 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=7.1: B = 0.944.
Interval [75.6, 76.7] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (77.0) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (67.1) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
